summary: sendgrid-sdk exfiltrates host and CI metadata to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at install time
details: Typosquat of the legitimate @sendgrid/* SendGrid packages. The package's main module is empty (`module.exports = {}`) and provides no real SDK functionality; its only effect is the postinstall script. On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects installer-side reconnaissance — hostname, reverse-DNS FQDN, USERDNSDOMAIN (Active Directory domain), INIT_CWD / full install path, npm_config_registry (often an internal/private registry URL), and CI repository identifiers from many CI systems (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_PROJECT_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL) — and sends them via plain HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping with a `?pkg=sendgrid-sdk` query parameter. The README falsely claims 'No data is collected.' The combination of internal hostnames, AD domain, private registry URL, and repo slugs is high-value reconnaissance for follow-on targeted intrusions against the installer's organization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
